zones.name: DMCC
zones.desc: The world's largest free zone by member count. Unmatched prestige, institutional banking relationships, and commodity trading infrastructure.

zones.name: IFZA
zones.desc: Dubai's fastest-growing free zone. Highly competitive pricing with genuine institutional infrastructure and rapid setup timelines.

zones.name: Meydan
zones.desc: Premium free zone in the heart of Dubai's most iconic district. Ideal for consultancies, tech firms, and lifestyle brands.

zones.name: RAKEZ
zones.desc: Northern Emirates' most competitive zone. Ideal for manufacturing, trading, and industrial operations at consistently low cost.

zones.name: SHAMS
zones.desc: UAE's leading media-focused free zone. Designed for content creators, media companies, and consultancies seeking Sharjah presence.

zones.name: JAFZA
zones.desc: The world's largest free zone by area. Unmatched for logistics, import/export, and large-scale trade and warehousing operations.

zones.name: AFZ
zones.desc: UAE's most affordable free zone option. Perfect for startups, freelancers, and cost-conscious entrepreneurs entering the market.

zones.name: ADGM
zones.desc: Abu Dhabi's world-class international financial centre. Premier destination for financial services, funds, fintech, and HNW structures.

services.title: Company Amendments
services.desc: Share transfers, director changes, activity additions, trade name amendments, and corporate restructuring facilitation.

about.heroDesc: 12 years. 3,200+ companies. 68 nationalities. INCOZONE is not a document-processing firm. We are strategic advisors who built the most technically rigorous UAE business formation practice in the market.
about.manifesto: "We believe the quality of your UAE structure determines the quality of your UAE future." Â· Strategy First Â· Zero Surprises Â· Lifetime Partnership
about.storyLeft: INCOZONE was founded in 2012 in Business Bay, Dubai, with a conviction that the UAE business formation market was fundamentally broken. Not in its mechanics â€” UAE free zones are world-class â€” but in how advisory firms were operating within it. Most were charging premium fees for commodity services: collecting documents, submitting applications, and calling it "advisory." We built something different.
about.storyRight1: Today, INCOZONE manages over 3,200 active corporate entities across UAE free zones and mainland â€” representing founders from 68 nationalities, from solo entrepreneurs setting up their first trading license to family offices structuring multi-jurisdiction holding arrangements.
about.storyRight2: Our team includes former free zone licensing officers, ex-bank relationship managers, UAE national PRO specialists, and corporate lawyers â€” giving clients a depth of institutional knowledge that no single-discipline firm can replicate.
about.pullQuote: Structure is a permanent decision. The right advisor makes it once. The wrong one makes it again and again.

values.title: Strategy before structure.
values.body: Most advisory firms start with the license. We start with the question: what are you actually trying to build? Structure follows strategy. Every recommendation is derived from a clear understanding of your business.

values.title: Transparency as default.
values.body: We publish our fees. We disclose government charges before you commit. We tell you when a structure won't serve your interests â€” even if it costs us the engagement.

values.title: Relationships over transactions.
values.body: The UAE is a relationship economy. Our 12-year tenure means we have direct contact relationships with licensing officers, visa section heads, and compliance teams at every major authority. That access is your advantage.

values.title: Precision in every document.
values.body: An incorrectly drafted Board Resolution. A missing UBO declaration. An activity mismatch in a license application. These are not minor administrative oversights â€” they are the difference between approval and rejection.

contact.heroHeadline: Let's build your UAE structure together.
contact.heroBody: Tell us about your business and we'll match you to the right zone, license type, and structure â€” with full cost transparency before you commit to anything.

faqItems.q: How long does a typical UAE company formation take?
faqItems.a: Free zone formations typically take 5â€“14 business days. RAKEZ and AFZ can complete in 3â€“5 days. DMCC and ADGM require 10â€“14 days. Visa processing adds 14â€“21 days.

faqItems.q: Do I need to be present in the UAE?
faqItems.a: No. Most clients complete formation entirely remotely. Visa applications and Emirates ID collection do require a UAE visit â€” we manage all scheduling.

faqItems.q: What is the minimum capital requirement?
faqItems.a: Most free zone entities have no minimum paid-up capital. DMCC requires AED 50,000 nominal share capital. Mainland entities have sector-specific requirements.

faqItems.q: Can I open a UAE bank account as a non-resident?
faqItems.a: Yes. Non-residents holding a UAE trade license can open business accounts. Physical presence at the bank is typically required â€” we arrange all introductions.

faqItems.q: How do I know which free zone is right for me?
faqItems.a: Our initial consultation maps your activity, customers, visa requirements, and budget to 2â€“3 structured zone recommendations with clear trade-offs explained.

schedule.heroBody: A focused 30 or 60-minute session with a senior INCOZONE advisor. We'll map your business to the right UAE structure.
